memory.summary: Mina and Jae are inviting me to Echo, but my midterm tomorrow makes this a risky distraction. I'll probably skip it to grind for the final hours before bed.

desires: Finish the midterm prep before tomorrow's deadline so I don't have to explain my poor performance later.
Get out of this study session ASAP without making anyone feel awkward about me being tired or distracted.

goals.description: Finish the midterm prep task completely so I don't have to explain my lack of focus tomorrow.
goals.rationale: The exam is the only thing that matters right now. Social plans are noise; I need to lock in the grade by resting so I can avoid panic later. Any delay risks my score.

goals.description: Reply to Theo saying 'Sure, I'll do it tomorrow' to shut down the laundry conversation without admitting I'm too tired or disorganized.
goals.rationale: I need to handle this after the study block. Pushing it back saves me from having to explain myself or deal with their disappointment right now. It's just extra work I don't want to do while my brain is tired.

goals.description: Draft a brief text to Mina confirming I will answer her question next week, keeping it vague enough to not look like avoidance.
goals.rationale: Mina expects an answer, but giving one now creates pressure and potential awkwardness if I'm not ready. A low-stakes deferral protects my social standing while I focus on the high-stakes exam. Better to be slightly evasive than visibly panicked.

agent.last_reasoning: Midterm prep is the only priority; grades matter most. I'll crush this now to avoid panic tomorrow. Social plans are noise and can wait until after I lock in my score.

goals.description: Finish the midterm review now (aim for 80% completion) and immediately stop to avoid last-minute panic tomorrow.
goals.rationale: Failing or looking unprepared is a non-negotiable failure I can't afford; my short planning horizon means I only care about securing that grade right now, not social obligations.

goals.description: Text Mina and Jae saying 'I'll pass on tonight' so they don't have to chase me down or put pressure on me in person.
goals.rationale: Defer until after study block. Handling their expectations feels like extra work/stress; I need to get this out of my head quickly before the energy drains further, but doing it now risks wasting time I need for the exam.

goals.description: Go home, change clothes, and crash for two hours straight.
goals.rationale: Only if I finish the study block early. My physical stamina is already low; pushing past the 2-hour crash would just make me lazy tomorrow, which contradicts my immediate goal of being ready for the exam.
